description: This line chart presents the relationship among the logarithm of the equilibrium constant (log K_TiN), the logarithm of the concentration product (log Q_TiN), and the solidification fraction (z_s) for TiN inclusion precipitation in Fe-15Mn-xAl-C low-density steels. Three alloys are compared: 1# (Fe-15Mn-1Al-C), 2# (Fe-15Mn-3Al-C), and 3# (Fe-15Mn-5Al-C). Solid curves denote log K_TiN, while dashed curves denote log Q_TiN for each sample. The intersections indicate the precipitation solidification fractions of TiN inclusions, which are 0.89 for 1#, 0.93 for 2#, and 0.94 for 3#. As Al content increases, the log Q_TiN curves shift upward and intersect the corresponding log K_TiN curves at progressively higher z_s values, demonstrating that higher Al content delays TiN precipitation during solidification. This trend is attributed to the altered solidification route and solute segregation behavior with increasing aluminum, as supported by FactSage 8.2 thermodynamic calculations.
